mission.title: caddy: Support Encrypted Client Hello (formerly known as ESNI)
mission.description: Support Encrypted Client Hello (formerly known as ESNI). This issue affects 59+ users.

steps.title: Identify caddy feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your caddy deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=caddy
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=caddy -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Support Encrypted Client Hello (formerly known as ESNI)

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant caddy resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
Please add support for [TLS Encrypted Client Hello](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-ietf-tls-esni/)

- Championed by the EFF: https://www.eff.org/deeplinks/2018/09/esni-privacy-protecting-upgra

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support Encrypted Client Hello (formerly known as ESNI)
steps.description:
Yeah; to clarify, we like ECH, and will support it someday assuming Go's standard lib implements it. (Because we're wary about switching to a fork of crypto/tls for our standard distribution of Caddy.)

However, I should note that ECH has limited practical use currently as DNS lookups still reveal hostnames in plaintext most of the time, and packets still have IP addresses that aren't encrypted.

It's just really, really hard to solve the problem people are hoping to solve, and ECH is not the wh

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm Support Encrypted Client Hello (formerly known as  is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=caddy -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: Yeah; to clarify, we like ECH, and will support it someday assuming Go's standard lib implements it. (Because we're wary about switching to a fork of crypto/tls for our standard distribution of Caddy.)

However, I should note that ECH has limited practical use currently as DNS lookups still reveal hostnames in plaintext most of the time, and packets still have IP addresses that aren't encrypted.

. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A working caddy installation or development environment.
